sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Right heel pain after activity
History of Present Illness
The patient reports right heel pain that occurs after activity, typically after a workout or when first getting up in the morning. It's a tight feeling and almost like tearing, and eases up after about 10-15 minutes of walking. It worsens when sitting for extended periods and then standing up again. He reports running approximately 10 kilometers three times a week, cycling for 45-60 minutes, and swimming for 30-60 minutes. He does not stretch and reports no formal stretching routine. He used to play hockey in Detroit but no longer participates.
Findings
Physical exam reveals tenderness to palpation of the right heel, positive Thompson's test, and pain with resisted dorsiflexion. Neurological exam is intact.
Assessment
Achilles tendinitis, likely secondary to overuse.
Plan
Prescribe ibuprofen 600mg every 6 hours as needed for pain. Order physical therapy evaluation and initiate a course of treatment focusing on stretching and strengthening exercises. Schedule a follow-up appointment in 7-10 days to assess progress. Recommend modification of training schedule until symptoms improve.
